title: COSO Principle 1 — Commitment to Integrity and Ethical Values
description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
purpose: Establishes the tone at the top and culture of integrity that underpins all internal controls.
implementationGuidance: Define and communicate a code of ethics. Establish mechanisms to report ethical violations. Demonstrate leadership commitment through behaviour and communication.
requiredDocuments: Code of Conduct / Code of Ethics
evidenceExamples: Signed Code of Conduct by all employees
Whistleblower hotline existence and usage records
bestPractices: Require annual Code of Conduct re-acknowledgement
Make reporting mechanisms confidential and non-retaliatory
Leadership should visibly reinforce ethical behaviour

title: COSO Principle 2 — Board Independence and Oversight
description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
purpose: Ensures executive management accountability through independent board oversight.
implementationGuidance: Establish a board with appropriate independence. Define board oversight responsibilities for cybersecurity and risk. Maintain regular security and risk reporting to the board.
requiredDocuments: Security risk reporting cadence to board
evidenceExamples: Board meeting minutes with security/risk agenda items
bestPractices: Establish a dedicated Risk or Audit Committee
Provide quarterly security briefings to the board
Engage independent security advisors for board support

title: COSO Principle 3 — Organisational Structure and Reporting Lines
description: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
purpose: Ensures clear accountability and authority for security management within the organisation.
implementationGuidance: Define an organisational structure with clear security responsibilities. Establish reporting lines from security team to executive leadership and board. Document roles and responsibilities in job descriptions.
evidenceExamples: CISO role description and reporting line documentation
bestPractices: Ensure CISO or equivalent has direct reporting access to the board
Maintain organisational chart current with personnel changes
Review security team structure annually for adequacy

title: COSO Principle 4 — Commitment to Competence
description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
purpose: Ensures the organisation has qualified people to operate security controls effectively.
implementationGuidance: Define competency requirements for security roles. Implement hiring, training, and retention practices that ensure security skills. Conduct performance evaluations that include security responsibilities.
requiredDocuments: Training and development plans for security staff
bestPractices: Require relevant certifications for security positions
Fund ongoing professional development for security staff
Conduct skills gap analysis annually for the security team

title: COSO Principle 5 — Accountability
description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
purpose: Ensures personnel are accountable for their security control responsibilities.
implementationGuidance: Include security responsibilities in job descriptions and performance evaluations. Establish consequences for control failures. Track and measure security control performance.
evidenceExamples: Records of accountability actions for control failures
bestPractices: Include security metrics in all relevant role performance reviews
Report security performance to leadership quarterly
Define clear consequences for wilful security policy violations

title: COSO Principle 13 — Relevant, Quality Information
description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.
purpose: Ensures decision-makers have accurate security information to manage risk effectively.
implementationGuidance: Implement systems to collect, process, and report security information to decision-makers. Define information quality standards. Ensure security metrics are accurate and timely.
bestPractices: Automate security metrics collection to reduce manual error
Define clear data sources and calculation methods for all metrics
Review security information quality in management reviews

title: COSO Principle 14 — Internal Communication
description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
purpose: Ensures all relevant personnel understand security objectives and their responsibilities.
implementationGuidance: Communicate security policies, objectives, and responsibilities to all relevant personnel. Use multiple channels (training, intranet, emails) for communication. Confirm receipt and understanding.
bestPractices: Use multiple channels to communicate security information
Tailor communications to different audiences (technical vs. general staff)
Measure comprehension through tests and surveys

title: COSO Principle 15 — External Communication
description: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
purpose: Ensures external parties are informed of relevant security requirements and incidents.
implementationGuidance: Define processes for communicating security information to customers, regulators, and partners. Establish incident notification procedures for external parties. Communicate security commitments in customer agreements.
bestPractices: Define SLAs for customer security breach notification
Maintain a current list of regulatory notification requirements
Designate a responsible person for all external security communications

title: COSO Principle 6 — Objectives Specification
description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.
purpose: Ensures security objectives are clear enough to enable meaningful risk assessment.
implementationGuidance: Define security objectives aligned with business objectives and regulatory requirements. Document objectives with measurable criteria. Review objectives as business needs change.
evidenceExamples: Alignment evidence between security and business objectives
bestPractices: Align security objectives with the overall business strategy
Make objectives measurable with defined KPIs
Review and update objectives annually

title: COSO Principle 7 — Risk Identification and Analysis
description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyses risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed.
purpose: Ensures threats to service security, availability, and confidentiality are systematically identified and assessed.
implementationGuidance: Conduct periodic risk assessments covering all relevant threat categories. Use a consistent risk rating methodology (likelihood × impact). Document identified risks in a risk register. Review risks when significant changes occur.
bestPractices: Conduct risk assessments at least annually
Include input from technical, operational, and business stakeholders
Use threat intelligence to inform risk identification

title: COSO Principle 8 — Fraud Risk Assessment
description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.
purpose: Ensures fraud risks (including insider threats) are considered in the organisation's risk management.
implementationGuidance: Include fraud and insider threat scenarios in risk assessments. Identify controls that mitigate fraud risk (segregation of duties, access reviews, monitoring). Train management to recognise fraud indicators.
evidenceExamples: Fraud risk scenarios in risk register
bestPractices: Assess fraud risk annually as part of the broader risk assessment
Implement technical controls to detect anomalous user behaviour
Establish an anonymous reporting mechanism for fraud suspicions

title: COSO Principle 9 — Identifying and Analysing Significant Change
description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.
purpose: Ensures significant changes are evaluated for their impact on security controls.
implementationGuidance: Implement a process to identify and assess significant changes (organisational, regulatory, technology, business model). Evaluate impact on security controls. Update risk assessments and controls as needed.
evidenceExamples: Risk assessment updates triggered by significant changes
Control update records following significant changes
bestPractices: Include security impact assessment in all major change management processes
Monitor external changes (regulation, threat landscape) for security impact
Re-assess risk when entering new markets or launching new products

title: COSO Principle 16 — Ongoing and Separate Evaluations
description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.
purpose: Ensures security controls are continuously monitored for effectiveness.
implementationGuidance: Implement continuous monitoring of security controls (automated where possible). Conduct periodic manual control evaluations. Use security metrics to track control effectiveness over time.
bestPractices: Automate control monitoring wherever possible using SIEM and GRC tools
Set thresholds for control performance metrics that trigger investigation
Report on control monitoring results to management monthly

title: COSO Principle 17 — Evaluating and Communicating Deficiencies
description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board of directors, as appropriate.
purpose: Ensures control deficiencies are promptly escalated and remediated.
implementationGuidance: Define a process to evaluate, classify, and communicate control deficiencies. Establish escalation thresholds for reporting to senior management and board. Track deficiencies to closure with defined remediation timelines.
evidenceExamples: Management and board reporting on deficiencies
bestPractices: Classify deficiencies by severity and define remediation SLAs by severity level
Report material deficiencies to the board within a defined timeframe
Track all deficiencies to closure and measure mean time to remediate

title: COSO Principle 10 — Selecting and Developing Control Activities
description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
purpose: Ensures appropriate controls are selected and designed to mitigate identified risks.
implementationGuidance: Select and design controls based on risk assessment outcomes. Ensure controls address identified threats to security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy. Document the rationale for control selection.
bestPractices: Map controls directly to identified risks to ensure coverage
Select preventive, detective, and corrective controls for layered defence
Review control adequacy when new risks are identified

title: COSO Principle 11 — General Technology Controls
description: The entity also selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
purpose: Ensures technology controls are in place to protect information systems.
implementationGuidance: Implement general IT controls covering logical access, change management, operations, and physical security. Ensure IT controls align with and support business process controls.
bestPractices: Document all general IT controls with clear testing criteria
Test IT general controls at least annually
Integrate IT controls with business process controls for comprehensive coverage

title: COSO Principle 12 — Deployment of Controls Through Policies and Procedures
description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
purpose: Ensures controls are operationalised through documented, enforced policies and procedures.
implementationGuidance: Translate control requirements into documented policies and operating procedures. Communicate policies to affected personnel. Verify procedures are followed through testing and monitoring.
bestPractices: Maintain all policies in a version-controlled repository accessible to all staff
Review and update procedures when systems or processes change
Test procedure effectiveness through walkthroughs and control testing

title: Logical Access Security Controls
description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures robust technical controls restrict access to information systems.
implementationGuidance: Implement logical access controls including authentication, authorisation, and session management. Enforce principle of least privilege. Use role-based access control. Implement multi-factor authentication for sensitive systems.
bestPractices: Enforce MFA for all user accounts accessing sensitive data or administrative functions
Implement RBAC aligned to job functions
Conduct quarterly access reviews to remove unnecessary permissions

title: User Registration, Authorisation, and Deprovisioning
description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorises new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.
purpose: Ensures only authorised users are granted access through a formal process.
implementationGuidance: Implement a formal user registration and access provisioning process. Require manager approval for access requests. Implement timely deprovisioning upon termination or role change.
bestPractices: Automate provisioning and deprovisioning through HR system integration
Audit active accounts monthly against current personnel list
Define maximum deprovisioning time (e.g., same day for terminations)

title: Role-Based Access and Segregation of Duties
description: The entity authorises, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties.
purpose: Prevents conflict of interest and ensures users only have access needed for their role.
implementationGuidance: Design and implement role-based access aligned to job functions. Identify and enforce segregation of duties for sensitive functions. Document the access control matrix. Review and validate role assignments regularly.
evidenceExamples: SOD conflict reports and resolution records
bestPractices: Automate SOD conflict detection using IGA tooling
Review and recertify all roles annually
Implement compensating controls where SOD cannot be fully enforced

title: Physical Access Controls
description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets (for example, data centre facilities, back-up media storage, and other sensitive locations) to authorised personnel to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Prevents unauthorised physical access to systems and data.
implementationGuidance: Implement physical access controls for data centres and server rooms. Use electronic access systems with audit logging. Manage visitor access with escort procedures. Review physical access rights regularly.
bestPractices: Implement biometric or multi-factor physical access for critical areas
Review physical access rights quarterly
Monitor and alert on after-hours physical access attempts

title: Secure Disposal of Assets
description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures data cannot be recovered from disposed assets.
implementationGuidance: Implement secure data sanitisation procedures before asset disposal or repurposing. Use NIST 800-88 sanitisation methods. Obtain and retain certificates of destruction. Remove logical access before physical disposal.
evidenceExamples: Asset disposal register with method used
bestPractices: Use certified data destruction services with certificate
Maintain a disposal log with asset ID, date, method, and certificate reference
Include disposal verification in the asset lifecycle process

title: Protection of System Components from Environmental Threats
description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorised or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Protects systems from malware, ransomware, and other malicious software threats.
implementationGuidance: Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools. Implement email and web filtering. Maintain anti-malware signatures up to date. Monitor for and alert on malware detection events. Define incident response for malware incidents.
bestPractices: Deploy next-gen EDR with behavioural detection capabilities
Maintain 100% endpoint protection coverage
Run automated malware scans on all file uploads and downloads

title: Encryption of Data in Transit and at Rest
description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorised internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures sensitive data is encrypted during transmission and at rest to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
implementationGuidance: Implement TLS 1.2 or higher for all data in transit. Encrypt sensitive data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent. Implement data loss prevention (DLP) controls. Monitor for sensitive data leaving approved channels.
requiredDocuments: Data in transit and at rest encryption standards
bestPractices: Achieve TLS 1.3 for all external communications
Implement full disk encryption on all endpoints
Deploy DLP to prevent unauthorised data exfiltration

title: Preventing and Detecting Malicious Code
description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorised or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Provides additional detection capabilities for sophisticated threats not caught by basic anti-malware.
implementationGuidance: Implement application whitelisting or allowlisting on critical systems. Deploy intrusion detection and prevention systems. Monitor for indicators of compromise. Implement security information and event management (SIEM).
bestPractices: Tune SIEM rules to reduce false positives while maintaining detection accuracy
Implement application control (allowlisting) for servers where feasible
Conduct threat hunting exercises to identify threats not caught by automated tools

title: System Vulnerability Detection and Monitoring
description: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
purpose: Ensures vulnerabilities are identified and assessed before they can be exploited.
implementationGuidance: Implement automated vulnerability scanning for all systems. Monitor vulnerability intelligence feeds. Define a process to assess and prioritise vulnerabilities. Report on open vulnerabilities and remediation progress.
evidenceExamples: Vulnerability tracking register with remediation status
bestPractices: Scan internet-facing systems daily, internal systems weekly
Prioritise vulnerabilities using CVSS and exploitability context
Track mean time to remediate (MTTR) by severity level

title: Security Event Monitoring and Alerting
description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives.
purpose: Enables rapid detection of security events through continuous monitoring.
implementationGuidance: Implement a SIEM to centralise and correlate log data. Define alerting rules for security events (failed logins, privilege escalation, data exfiltration). Establish 24/7 monitoring capability. Define response procedures for each alert type.
bestPractices: Implement around-the-clock monitoring for critical systems
Define response playbooks for common alert types
Regularly tune SIEM rules to reduce alert fatigue

title: Incident Response and Recovery
description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
purpose: Ensures security incidents are managed in a structured, effective manner.
implementationGuidance: Define and implement an incident response plan. Establish an incident response team with clear roles. Define incident classification and escalation criteria. Conduct incident response exercises at least annually.
bestPractices: Test the incident response plan with at least one tabletop exercise per year
Define escalation thresholds and communication templates in advance
Conduct blameless post-mortems after every significant incident

title: Incident Identification and Root Cause Analysis
description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response programme to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate about security incidents, as appropriate.
purpose: Ensures incidents are thoroughly investigated to enable effective remediation and prevention of recurrence.
implementationGuidance: Conduct root cause analysis for all significant security incidents. Document findings and contributing factors. Define action items to address root causes. Share lessons learned with relevant stakeholders.
evidenceExamples: Action item tracking for incident learnings
bestPractices: Use structured RCA methodologies (5 Whys, fishbone diagram)
Conduct post-incident reviews within 5 business days of incident closure
Track all action items to closure and measure completion rates

title: Incident Recovery Procedures
description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
purpose: Ensures the organisation can effectively restore normal operations following a security incident.
implementationGuidance: Define recovery procedures for different incident types. Test recovery capabilities including restoring from backups and rebuilding compromised systems. Define recovery time objectives (RTO) for critical systems.
bestPractices: Define and test recovery procedures for most likely incident scenarios
Regularly test backup restoration to verify recoverability
Document RTO/RPO for all critical systems and verify achievement in tests

title: Change Management
description: The entity authorises, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
purpose: Ensures changes to systems and infrastructure are controlled to prevent security incidents.
implementationGuidance: Implement a formal change management process with authorisation, testing, and rollback planning. Apply emergency change procedures for urgent changes. Maintain a change log. Conduct post-implementation reviews.
evidenceExamples: Change tickets with complete approval chain
bestPractices: Use an ITSM platform to track all changes end-to-end
Require separate development and operational sign-off for production changes
Conduct post-implementation reviews for all significant changes

title: Risk Mitigation Activities
description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
purpose: Ensures the organisation has plans and controls to mitigate identified risks to business continuity.
implementationGuidance: Develop risk mitigation strategies for identified risks (avoidance, reduction, transfer, acceptance). Implement controls for risk reduction. Purchase appropriate insurance for risk transfer. Document risk acceptance decisions with management sign-off.
evidenceExamples: Risk treatment plan with mitigating controls
Business continuity and disaster recovery plans
bestPractices: Review and update risk treatment plans when the risk landscape changes
Ensure risk acceptance decisions are made at the appropriate management level
Test risk mitigation controls through exercises and simulations

title: Vendor and Business Partner Risk Management
description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
purpose: Ensures third-party risks are identified, assessed, and managed.
implementationGuidance: Implement a third-party risk management programme. Conduct security assessments of vendors before engagement and periodically thereafter. Include security requirements in vendor contracts. Monitor vendor risk continuously.
bestPractices: Tier vendors by risk level and frequency of assessment
Request SOC 2 reports or equivalent from critical vendors
Monitor vendors for security incidents through third-party risk intelligence services

title: System Availability Capacity Planning
description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives.
purpose: Ensures the system has sufficient capacity to meet agreed service levels.
implementationGuidance: Monitor capacity utilisation of all system components. Set alerts for capacity thresholds. Conduct capacity planning reviews and forecast future requirements. Scale capacity proactively.
bestPractices: Monitor capacity continuously with automated alerts
Conduct capacity planning reviews quarterly
Use cloud auto-scaling to handle demand spikes automatically

title: System Availability Backup and Recovery
description: The entity authorises, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data back-up processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
purpose: Ensures data and systems can be recovered in the event of failure or disaster.
implementationGuidance: Implement automated backups with defined frequency and retention. Store backups offsite or in a separate cloud region. Test restoration at least quarterly. Define and test RTO/RPO for critical systems.
bestPractices: Follow 3-2-1 backup rule
Test full system restoration at least annually
Document and verify RTO/RPO achievement in DR tests

title: System Availability Recovery Testing
description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
purpose: Verifies that recovery procedures work as expected before a real disaster occurs.
implementationGuidance: Conduct regular DR tests covering backup restoration, failover to secondary systems, and communication procedures. Document test results. Identify and address gaps. Update DR plans based on test findings.
requiredDocuments: DR test results and gap analysis
evidenceExamples: Test results with RTO/RPO achievement evidence
bestPractices: Conduct at least one full DR test per year
Include business stakeholders in DR tests to verify business process recovery
Update the DR plan within 30 days of test completion

title: Processing Integrity — System Input Controls
description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product and service specifications, to support the use of products and services.
purpose: Ensures data input into systems is complete, accurate, and authorised.
implementationGuidance: Implement input validation controls on all data entry points. Define data quality standards. Implement error detection and correction procedures. Validate data against business rules before processing.
bestPractices: Implement both client-side and server-side input validation
Log and monitor input errors for anomaly detection
Define data quality metrics and report on them regularly

title: Processing Integrity — System Processing Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness and accuracy, to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures system processing operates correctly and completely.
implementationGuidance: Implement processing controls including transaction logging, reconciliation, and exception handling. Conduct regular reconciliation of processed data. Monitor processing completeness and accuracy.
bestPractices: Automate reconciliation where possible
Monitor processing completion with automated alerts for failures
Investigate and resolve exceptions within defined SLAs

title: Processing Integrity — System Output Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system outputs, including controls over completeness and accuracy, to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures system outputs are accurate, complete, and delivered to authorised recipients.
implementationGuidance: Implement output validation controls. Verify outputs against expected results. Control distribution of outputs to authorised recipients. Log and monitor all output generation and distribution.
evidenceExamples: Reconciliation of expected vs. actual outputs
bestPractices: Validate critical outputs against expected values automatically
Restrict output distribution to authorised recipients only
Log all output generation and distribution for audit trail

title: Processing Integrity — Error Identification and Correction
description: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures processing errors are detected and corrected promptly.
implementationGuidance: Implement error detection and notification systems. Define error correction procedures. Log and track all processing errors. Analyse error trends to identify systemic issues.
bestPractices: Implement automated error detection with immediate notification
Define SLAs for error correction by severity
Use error patterns to drive system improvements

title: Processing Integrity — Storage Integrity
description: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with system specifications to meet the entity's objectives.
purpose: Ensures stored data maintains its integrity over time.
implementationGuidance: Implement controls to protect stored data from corruption, loss, and unauthorised modification. Use checksums or hashing for data integrity verification. Implement WORM storage for critical records.
bestPractices: Implement automated integrity checking on critical data stores
Use immutable storage for compliance records
Monitor for unauthorised data modifications with alerting

title: Confidentiality — Identification and Classification
description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
purpose: Ensures confidential information is identified so appropriate protections can be applied.
implementationGuidance: Define what constitutes confidential information. Implement information classification procedures. Label confidential information. Maintain an inventory of confidential information assets.
bestPractices: Use automated classification tools to identify and label confidential data
Conduct periodic audits of confidential data locations
Train staff on identifying and handling confidential information

title: Confidentiality — Disposal of Confidential Information
description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
purpose: Ensures confidential information is securely disposed of when no longer needed.
implementationGuidance: Define retention and disposal procedures for confidential information. Apply secure deletion or physical destruction. Obtain and retain certificates of destruction. Monitor disposal compliance.
bestPractices: Automate secure deletion where possible
Maintain audit trail of all confidential information disposal
Include confidential data disposal in the asset disposal process

title: Privacy Notice
description: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures individuals are informed about how their personal data is collected and used.
implementationGuidance: Develop and publish a privacy notice that clearly explains data collection practices, purposes, sharing, and individual rights. Keep the notice current and easily accessible. Update when practices change.
bestPractices: Write privacy notices in plain language accessible to the average person
Review and update privacy notice annually or when practices change
Implement layered notices for complex data processing

title: Privacy Choice and Consent
description: The entity communicates choices available regarding the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information to the data subjects and the consequences, if any, of each choice.
purpose: Ensures individuals can make informed choices about how their personal data is used.
implementationGuidance: Implement consent mechanisms for data processing activities requiring consent. Provide opt-out options for optional data uses (e.g., marketing). Record and honour consent and preference choices. Allow withdrawal of consent easily.
evidenceExamples: Consent records with timestamp and scope
bestPractices: Implement a consent management platform (CMP)
Make consent withdrawal as easy as giving consent
Audit consent records periodically for completeness and accuracy

title: Privacy Collection — Limitation
description: Personal information is collected consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures only necessary personal data is collected.
implementationGuidance: Collect only personal data necessary for the defined purpose (data minimisation). Review data collection practices against purposes. Remove collection of data no longer needed. Assess collection practices during DPIA.
evidenceExamples: Data collection inventory showing purpose justification
DPIA records for data collection activities
bestPractices: Apply data minimisation as a design principle for all new systems
Conduct annual reviews of data collected to identify excess collection
Challenge each data collection field with 'is this strictly necessary?'

title: Privacy Collection — Sensitive Personal Information
description: For information requiring explicit consent, the entity obtains explicit consent for the collection of sensitive personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures explicit consent is obtained for sensitive personal data collection.
implementationGuidance: Identify categories of sensitive personal data (health, biometric, racial/ethnic origin, etc.). Implement explicit consent mechanisms for sensitive data collection. Apply enhanced protections for sensitive data.
evidenceExamples: Explicit consent records for sensitive data collection
Enhanced security controls for sensitive data stores
DPIA records for sensitive data processing
bestPractices: Map all sensitive data processing and apply enhanced controls
Require explicit (not implicit) consent for sensitive data
Conduct DPIAs for all sensitive data processing activities

title: Privacy Use — Limitation
description: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures personal data is only used for stated, consented purposes.
implementationGuidance: Define permitted uses of personal data by type and purpose. Implement access controls to restrict use to authorised purposes. Monitor data usage for compliance with stated purposes.
evidenceExamples: Access controls restricting use by purpose
bestPractices: Implement purpose-based access controls in data systems
Conduct periodic reviews of data use against stated purposes
Alert on data access outside normal patterns indicating possible misuse

title: Privacy Retention — Schedule
description: The entity retains personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures personal data is retained only as long as necessary.
implementationGuidance: Define retention periods for all categories of personal data aligned with legal requirements and business needs. Implement automated deletion or anonymisation at end of retention period. Document retention justification.
bestPractices: Automate deletion of personal data at end of retention period
Align retention schedules with all applicable legal requirements
Review retention periods annually for continued appropriateness

title: Privacy Disposal
description: The entity disposes of personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures personal data is securely disposed of when retention period ends.
implementationGuidance: Implement secure disposal of personal data including digital deletion (cryptographic erasure, overwriting) and physical destruction. Verify disposal completeness. Extend disposal requirements to processors and subprocessors.
bestPractices: Use cryptographic erasure for cloud-stored data
Require processor confirmation of data disposal
Maintain an audit trail of all personal data disposal activities

title: Privacy Access — Individual Rights
description: The entity grants identified and authenticated data subjects the ability to access their stored personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies of that information to data subjects.
purpose: Enables individuals to exercise their right to access their personal data.
implementationGuidance: Implement processes to receive, verify, and fulfil data subject access requests (DSARs). Define response timelines (e.g., 30 days under GDPR). Verify requestor identity before providing data. Log all access requests.
evidenceExamples: DSAR tracking log with response times
bestPractices: Implement a self-service portal for data subject requests where feasible
Define and meet 30-day response target (or jurisdiction-specific requirement)
Track DSAR volume, type, and response time as a privacy metric

title: Privacy Correction — Individual Rights
description: The entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by data subjects and communicates related information to third parties, if any, consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Enables individuals to exercise their right to correct inaccurate personal data.
implementationGuidance: Implement processes to receive and fulfil data correction requests. Verify the correction is accurate before implementing. Propagate corrections to processors and other parties where data was shared.
bestPractices: Implement data correction workflows in all major data systems
Log all corrections with before/after state for audit trail
Notify all processors of data corrections within a defined timeframe

title: Privacy Disclosure — Authorised Purposes
description: The entity discloses personal information to third parties with the explicit consent of data subjects or for purposes consistent with those identified in the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures personal data is only shared with third parties for authorised purposes with appropriate consent.
implementationGuidance: Maintain an inventory of all third-party data sharing. Obtain consent or document legal basis for each sharing arrangement. Implement contractual requirements (DPAs) for all processors. Monitor third-party data access.
bestPractices: Maintain a current and comprehensive data sharing register
Execute DPAs with all processors before sharing personal data
Review third-party sharing arrangements annually

title: Privacy Disclosure — Third-Party Obligations
description: The entity creates and retains a complete and accurate record of authorised disclosures of personal information.
purpose: Maintains accountability records for all personal data disclosures.
implementationGuidance: Log all personal data disclosures to third parties. Record the purpose, recipient, data categories, and legal basis. Retain disclosure records for defined periods. Make available to data subjects upon DSAR.
evidenceExamples: Audit trail of third-party data transfers
bestPractices: Automate disclosure logging through data transfer monitoring tools
Retain disclosure records for the minimum required period plus buffer
Include disclosure history in DSAR responses as required by law

title: Privacy Disclosure — Notification to Third Parties
description: The entity obtains privacy commitments from vendors and other parties who have access to personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures third parties who handle personal data make equivalent privacy commitments.
implementationGuidance: Require all vendors and partners with access to personal data to make privacy commitments contractually. Include privacy requirements in procurement. Conduct due diligence on vendor privacy practices.
bestPractices: Include privacy requirements in vendor RFP and procurement processes
Assess vendor privacy practices before engagement and annually
Require vendors to notify of privacy-impacting changes to their services

title: Privacy Disclosure — Privacy Incident Notification
description: The entity notifies data subjects, authorities, and other parties about actual or possible unauthorized access to personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures proper notification when personal data is involved in a breach.
implementationGuidance: Define breach notification procedures including criteria for notification, timelines, content, and channels. Identify notification obligations under all applicable regulations. Maintain templates for notifications.
evidenceExamples: Regulatory notifications sent within required timeframes
bestPractices: Define notification timelines for each applicable jurisdiction (e.g., 72 hours for GDPR)
Pre-draft notification templates to speed response during an incident
Test breach notification procedures in incident response exercises

title: Privacy Disclosure — Legal and Regulatory Disclosures
description: The entity obtains confidentiality commitments that are consistent with the entity's privacy objectives from vendors and other parties who have access to personal information.
purpose: Ensures legal and regulatory disclosure obligations are met.
implementationGuidance: Identify all legal and regulatory disclosure requirements. Implement processes to respond to lawful requests for personal data. Document each disclosure with legal basis and recipient. Seek legal advice when requests are unclear or potentially unlawful.
evidenceExamples: Legal disclosure records with documented legal basis
bestPractices: Establish a process for reviewing and challenging potentially unlawful requests
Log all law enforcement requests and responses
Notify data subjects of law enforcement requests where legally permissible

title: Privacy Disclosure — Onward Transfer
description: The entity obtains privacy commitments consistent with the entity's privacy objectives from vendors and other parties who have access to personal information.
purpose: Ensures personal data is protected when transferred to sub-processors or other parties.
implementationGuidance: Implement controls to govern onward transfer of personal data by processors. Require processors to obtain approval before engaging sub-processors. Ensure onward transfers to third countries comply with applicable transfer mechanisms.
evidenceExamples: International transfer mechanism evidence (SCCs, adequacy decisions)
bestPractices: Maintain an up-to-date list of all sub-processors and their locations
Implement SCCs or other appropriate transfer mechanisms for international transfers
Audit sub-processor compliance with privacy commitments periodically

title: Privacy Disclosure — Accurate and Complete Records
description: The entity provides data subjects with an accounting of the personal information held and, upon their request, provides individuals with access to their personal information.
purpose: Enables data subjects to understand and access all personal data held about them.
implementationGuidance: Maintain accurate records of all personal data processed per data subject. Be able to respond to access requests with a complete view of personal data held. Implement data mapping to support this.
requiredDocuments: Personal data inventory by data subject
evidenceExamples: Data mapping showing personal data per system
bestPractices: Implement data discovery tools to maintain a complete personal data inventory
Test DSAR responses periodically to verify completeness
Integrate data mapping with DSAR processes for efficient fulfilment

title: Privacy Quality — Accurate, Complete, Relevant Data
description: The entity collects and maintains personal information that is accurate, complete, and relevant for the purposes identified in the notice and consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
purpose: Ensures personal data quality is maintained to protect individuals from harm caused by inaccurate data.
implementationGuidance: Implement data quality controls for personal data. Provide mechanisms for individuals to correct their data. Conduct periodic data quality audits. Define data quality standards for personal data.
bestPractices: Implement automated data quality checks on personal data inputs
Provide self-service data correction mechanisms for individuals
Conduct annual data quality audits on critical personal data stores

title: Privacy Monitoring and Enforcement
description: The entity monitors compliance with its privacy objectives and procedures and addresses privacy-related complaints and disputes.
purpose: Ensures ongoing compliance with privacy commitments and effective handling of privacy complaints.
implementationGuidance: Implement privacy monitoring programme including regular audits and assessments. Establish a privacy complaint handling process. Designate a privacy contact (DPO or equivalent). Track and remediate privacy violations.
bestPractices: Conduct annual privacy programme assessments
Define SLAs for complaint response and resolution
Report on privacy metrics (complaints, violations, remediation) to leadership
